title: dactylorhiza baumanniana subsp. smolikana
intro: Dactylorhiza baumanniana subsp. smolikana, also known as Smolik's Marsh Orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Eastern Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza baumanniana subsp. smolikana include its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use as a medicinal plant requires further research and caution.
